p.strong: Why WAL is Necessary for Durability

div.p: Steal = YES Uncommitted changes CAN be written to disk
Force = NO Commit does NOT flush pages to disk

div.h2: After Crash, Disk State is Uncertain:

g.text: Uncommitted data | May be present (steal wrote it)
Committed data | May be MISSING (no-force didn't flush)
Problem | No way to distinguish or fix without WAL
Buffer pool evicts Page 5 → disk has B
Recovery: disk has B, but T1 committed
Without WAL: Is B correct? Corrupted? Unknown!
UNDO Rule | Log before-image BEFORE page write | Enables rollback of uncommitted
REDO Rule | Log after-image BEFORE commit | Enables replay of committed
WAL Protocol | Log record must be on disk BEFORE data page
REDO: Replay all logged changes (committed + uncommitted)
UNDO: Roll back uncommitted using before-images
